Issue: PatchEmbeddingBlock with proj_type='perceptron' raises TypeError on einops >= 0.8.x due to Rearrange.__init__() no longer accepting arbitrary keyword arguments
Decision: Embed patch size values directly as integer literals in the einops pattern string (e.g., 'b c (h 16) (w 16) (d 16)') instead of using kwargs
Rationale: einops supports integer literals in patterns across all versions including 0.8.x, making the change semantically identical to the original implementation
Author: williams145
Description: PatchEmbeddingBlock in MONAI's codebase, specifically the proj_type='perceptron' implementation
Cost: Low (non-breaking change fix with minimal code modification)
Risk: Technical debt from maintaining compatibility with multiple einops versions, potential side effects from pattern string changes

Argument.author: coderabbitai[bot]
Argument.argument: Introduces a new PyTorch module _PatchRearrange that converts inputs shaped (B, C, ...spatial...) into a sequence (B, n_patches, patch_dim) using explicit view/permute/reshape. In PatchEmbeddingBlock.__init__ for proj_type == 'perceptron', construction of the Rearrage layer is wrapped in a try block; if einops.Rearrange raises a TypeError, code falls back to _PatchRearrange(spatial_dims, patch_size) before applying nn.Linear(self.patch_dim, hidden_size). The original Rearrage -> Linear path is preserved when no error occurs.

Argument.author: williams145
Argument.argument: CI is green and the branch is up to date, indicating successful testing of the fix
